vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding current biological presence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are represented by a single, struggling seedling. P3 (Pothos) is the only specimen showing consistent, albeit limited, vitality.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Pothos leaf with white rabbit anchor)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: New Introduction/Intervention (Single seedling in black pot)

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Cluster: Contains a single, thin-stemmed seedling. No evidence of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest.
plant_audit.P3: Single Pothos leaf remains, appears stable but isolated.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, appears to have high organic content. No fungal blooms detected.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of eggshell fragments (calcium amendment) in the black pot. White cable clutter introduced in later images.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Seedling in P4 shows slight elongation.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Seedling exhibits minor postural shift; potential phototropic response to ambient light.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: Lighting conditions shifted to low-spectrum/night mode (T-1), returning to cool LED in current frame. Seedling remains static.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe depletion. P1 and P2 are effectively dead or failed. The single seedling in P4 is the only active biological indicator, though its health is precarious due to lack of foliage mass. P3 is dormant/stagnant.
anomalies: Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment.
White cable introduced into the frame at T-2.
Significant discrepancy between registry and physical reality.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment has suffered a near-total collapse of the original registry. The yellow pot (P1) is barren. The black pots contain only a solitary, fragile seedling and a single Pothos leaf. The environment is currently more of a soil-monitoring station than a thriving garden, with the white rabbit anchor serving as the only constant in an otherwise depleted system.
